Title: Engineering hairy root cultures to Gypsophila elegans produce endosomal escape-enhancing saponins

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports catalytic activity of heterologously expressed S. officinalis CYP450s and thereby enables accumulation of oxidized triterpene intermediates and QA-related products in yeast.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression support in yeast pathway reconstitution
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtCPR1 was required as redox partner for efficient CYP450 function in yeast. 2. It enabled oxidation steps catalyzed by SoCYP716A378, SoCYP716A379, SoCYP72A984, and SoCYP72A1003. 3. It is a supporting but not focal biosynthetic determinant.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenically expressed in yeast under galactose-inducible promoter
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as heterologous partner enzyme for CYP activity rather than investigated as primary research target.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enabling component for microbial reconstitution of plant P450 pathways.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Boosts precursor flux through the mevalonate/triterpene pathway in yeast to support production of target triterpene aglycones.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression support in yeast pathway reconstitution
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A feedback-insensitive truncated HMGR1 was co-expressed to enhance precursor supply in yeast. 2. It served as a flux booster for heterologous triterpene biosynthesis. 3. It was not a focal causal gene analyzed for metabolite specificity.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenically expressed in yeast under galactose-inducible promoter
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Support gene used to ensure consistent pathway flux.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful chassis-engineering component for microbial triterpene production.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Gypsophila elegans; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: BY4742-derived strain BY4272 lineage; NA; NA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Galactose-inducible promoter in yeast; CaMV 35S for Nicotiana localisation; CaMV 35S in single-gene hairy root constructs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the committed triterpene scaffold β-amyrin for downstream SO1861/quillaic acid-related saponin biosynthesis; functional in yeast but overexpression with CYP set in G. elegans did not markedly shift profile beyond controls.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oleanane/β-amyrin branch leading to quillaic acid (QA) and SO1861
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene into the pentacyclic triterpene β-amyrin.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hairy roots in applied Gypsophila system; naturally saponin-related transcripts highest in JA-treated roots of source species
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Endosomal escape-enhancing saponin useful for delivery of DNA/protein therapeutics and as adjuvant-related high-value molecule
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly JA responsive; highest in JA-treated roots at 6 h; constitutively present in aerial parts under non-elicited conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenically expressed in yeast, N. benthamiana leaves, and G. elegans hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate treatment induces transcript accumulation in S. officinalis; galactose induction in yeast culture
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript co-expression analysis; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae with GC-MS; transient expression/localisation in Nicotiana benthamiana; hairy root engineering in Gypsophila elegans
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SoBAS was identified in a JA-responsive co-expression cluster linked to SO1861 biosynthesis. 2. Yeast expression confirmed production of β-amyrin. 3. It supplies the committed precursor used by downstream CYP450s for QA/SO1861-related aglycone formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful entry-point gene for reconstructing oleanane saponin pathways, but alone is insufficient; should be stacked with oxidation and likely glycosylation genes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combinatorial expression with multiple CYPs in G. elegans did not automatically increase target product, indicating pathway competition/network complexity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AlphaFold3 suggested no clear transmembrane domain, yet microscopy indicated ER association, possibly via interaction with membrane-bound CYP450s.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Gypsophila elegans; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Galactose-inducible promoter in yeast; CaMV 35S for Nicotiana localisation; CaMV 35S in single-gene hairy root constructs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes C-28 oxidation of β-amyrin to erythrodiol and oleanolic acid, supplying oxidized intermediates for QA/SO1861 aglycone formation; alone did not substantially alter G. elegans saponin profile.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor / upstream oxidized intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Sequentially oxidizes the C-28 position of β-amyrin, producing erythrodiol and then oleanolic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hairy roots in applied Gypsophila system
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: JA responsive, highest in JA-treated roots at 6 h
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenically expressed in yeast, N. benthamiana leaves, and G. elegans hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate induction in source plant; galactose induction in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Sequence homology; prior transient expression in N. benthamiana; yeast combinatorial expression with GC-MS; GFP localisation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SoCYP716A378 oxidizes β-amyrin at C-28. 2. In yeast it produced erythrodiol and oleanolic acid with BAS and CPR. 3. In combination with SoCYP72A984 it contributed to gypsogenin/gypsogenic acid formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked to build QA-derived saponin pathways, but expression alone is insufficient to drive SO1861 accumulation in G. elegans.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Redundancy with endogenous G. elegans enzymes may mask phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously characterized in Nicotiana; behaved consistently in yeast and plant transient assays.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Gypsophila elegans; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Galactose-inducible promoter in yeast; CaMV 35S for Nicotiana localisation; CaMV 35S in single-gene hairy root constructs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Performs dual C-28/C-16α oxidation on β-amyrin-derived intermediates; contributes to QA backbone formation in combination with C-23 oxidases, but alone did not shift G. elegans saponin profile strongly.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor / modifies backbone toward target aglycone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Echinocystic acid; contributes to QA-related intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes triterpene backbone at C-16α and also C-28, generating echinocystic acid and related intermediates required for quillaic acid-type aglycones.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hairy roots in applied Gypsophila system
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: JA responsive, highest in JA-treated roots at 6 h
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenically expressed in yeast, N. benthamiana leaves, and G. elegans hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate induction in source plant; galactose induction in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior transient expression in N. benthamiana; yeast combinatorial expression with GC-MS and LC-MS; GFP localisation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SoCYP716A379 is a dual-function oxidase active at C-28 and C-16α. 2. In yeast it generated echinocystic acid. 3. Together with both C-23 oxidases it enabled production of quillaic acid in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important stacking component for engineering QA/SO1861 aglycones; requires matching C-23 oxidation context.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined expression with all pathway CYPs in G. elegans did not increase target output, suggesting network buffering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Dual functionality confirmed in both yeast and Nicotiana systems.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Gypsophila elegans; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Galactose-inducible promoter in yeast; CaMV 35S for Nicotiana localisation; CaMV 35S in single-gene hairy root constructs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Canonical C-23 oxidase that increases QA/SO1861 precursor-type saponins in G. elegans and produces C-23 oxidized aglycones in yeast, but alone tends toward overoxidation to gypsogenic-acid-type products rather than stable QA.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Suggestive key constraint point for QA-derived saponins in G. elegans
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Branch-determining oxidation step toward QA-derived saponins
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: C-28 oxidized oleanane intermediates / β-amyrin-derived intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Gypsogenin; gypsogenic acid; QA-precursor-level oxidation states
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes the C-23 position of oleanane triterpenes; in heterologous systems can proceed beyond aldehyde to the carboxylic acid state.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hairy roots in applied Gypsophila system
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly JA responsive with highest expression in JA-treated roots at 6 h
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenically expressed in yeast, N. benthamiana leaves, and G. elegans hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate induction in source plant; galactose induction in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression clustering; prior transient expression in N. benthamiana; yeast combinatorial expression with GC-MS/LC-MS; GFP localisation; hairy root metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SoCYP72A984 is a canonical C-23 oxidase co-expressed with SoBAS. 2. In yeast, with SoCYP716A378 it produced gypsogenin and gypsogenic acid-type products rather than clear QA. 3. In G. elegans hairy roots it increased precursor-like peaks and gypsophilosid accumulation, highlighting C-23 oxidation as a major control point.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to boost QA-derived precursor flux, but may require co-expression with non-canonical partner(s) or downstream glycosylation/competing-pathway edits to avoid overoxidation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overoxidation to C-23 carboxyl products may divert flux away from QA; strong endogenous pathway competition in hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Simultaneous expression with SoCYP72A1003 and SoCYP716A379 was needed to detect a peak matching QA in yeast.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Gypsophila elegans; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Galactose-inducible promoter in yeast; CaMV 35S for Nicotiana localisation; CaMV 35S in single-gene hairy root constructs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Non-canonical/truncated C-23 oxidase that modulates C-23 oxidation toward QA production in yeast and uniquely enabled accumulation of a peak matching SO1861 in G. elegans hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supports C-23 oxidation as major constraint/branch-control step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Branch-determining oxidation step directing flux toward QA and SO1861
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Oleanane triterpene intermediates, likely C-28/C-16 oxidized substrates in combination contexts
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: QA-favoring C-23 oxidation products; SO1861-associated product in hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-23 oxidation of oleanane triterpenes, apparently with altered oxidation behavior that favors accumulation of aldehyde-level QA when combined with other pathway enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in JA-treated leaves at 6 h and 24 h; tissue-specific aerial response distinct from other pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenically expressed in yeast, N. benthamiana leaves, and G. elegans hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate induction in source plant; galactose induction in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 CYP72 family; truncated/non-canonical form lacking apparent N-terminal transmembrane domain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TBLASTN discovery; expression profiling; yeast combinatorial expression with GC-MS/LC-MS; GFP localisation; hairy root metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SoCYP72A1003 is a newly discovered non-canonical CYP72 homologue with distinct JA-responsive aerial expression. 2. In yeast, together with SoCYP72A984 and SoCYP716A379, it enabled quillaic acid production. 3. In G. elegans hairy roots, expression of SoCYP72A1003 alone yielded a peak matching the SO1861 standard.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering gene for producing EEE saponins in heterologous hairy roots; attractive for mix-and-match pathway design and possibly for rewiring latent pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Truncated nature may reflect incomplete transcript; altered localisation/solubility may cause context dependence or unpredictable enzyme interactions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Lacks canonical transmembrane anchor and shows diffuse localization; may act through altered competition or interaction with membrane-bound enzymes to prevent overoxidation.
